summary: Matched a first substantial slice of src/melee/gr/granime.c: 8.74 KB at 37.77% file score, adding 481 lines of C for stage animation helper routines and marking the corresponding scratch entries OK. The PR also adjusted supporting headers so the new code could access heap free semantics and PObj shape-animation AObj storage.
what_changed: Created the body of granime.c from an effectively empty file, including includes for lb_00B0, lbarchive, lbheap, and baselib DObj/MObj/RObj/TObj headers.

Added matched implementations for grAnime_801C65B0, grAnime_801C6620, grAnime_801C6710, grAnime_801C67A8, grAnime_801C683C, grAnime_801C68F4, grAnime_801C6960, grAnime_801C69FC, grAnime_801C6A54, grAnime_801C6C0C, fn_801C6EE4, fn_801C6F2C, grAnime_801C6F50, grAnime_801C706C, grAnime_801C70E0, and grAnime_801C7228.
Implemented animation-loading traversal over JObj, DObj, MObj, PObj, TObj, and RObj chains, using HSD_AObjRemove and HSD_AObjLoadDesc when new animation descriptors exist.
Implemented callback dispatch over animation objects through grAnime_801C6F50 with several function-pointer typedefs and type-specific argument handling.
Added grAnime_804D6958 and grAnime_804D695C globals used as rewind/end frame values in fn_801C6EE4.
Updated scratches.txt entries from percentage values to OK for functions in the 0x801C5FC0-0x801C7640 granime range, including entries not all shown as newly implemented in the excerpt.
Changed lbHeap_80015CA8 prototype from taking u32* to void*, matching its use with archive/object pointers in grAnime_801C65B0.
Extended baselib/pobj.h with struct _unk_struct_pobj containing an HSD_AObj* after data[8], and added a union member unk so granime can access pobj->u.unk->aobj.
smart_moves: Used small inline helpers such as HSD_TexAnimFindById and grAnime_801C6710_all to express repeated linked-list traversal while still supporting matching.
Modeled traversal in lockstep across object and animation-joint sibling lists, consistently advancing nullable animjoint, matanimjoint, and shapeanimjoint pointers with ternary NULL checks.
Kept known-but-unnamed data structures honest by using provisional names like Foo and _unk_struct_pobj instead of overclaiming semantics.
Introduced explicit enum constants for object argument types and derived callback bitmasks, making magic bits like DObj/MObj/PObj/TObj callback targeting easier to follow.
Used uintptr_t in grAnime_801C65B0 to compare an archive pointer against the sentinel -1U without relying on an invalid pointer-typed comparison.
Preserved matching-sensitive alternatives with #if 0 blocks and used #pragma dont_inline around a call site in grAnime_801C683C, indicating deliberate control of compiler output.
Expanded shared headers only as far as needed for this match: one heap prototype generalization and one PObj union view for shape-animation AObj access.
decomp_lessons: For baselib animation code, matching often requires representing parallel object trees and animation descriptor trees as nullable linked lists advanced in lockstep.
AObj replacement patterns are recurring: check descriptor, remove an existing aobj if present, then load the new descriptor and store it back on the owning object.
When a structure union is still ambiguous, adding a narrowly scoped unknown struct/member can unblock matching while documenting the exact observed layout.
Scratch entries should be marked OK once the repository source supersedes scratch percentages, even if old scratch names include duplicates or forked attempts.
Callback dispatcher functions can be matched more readably by typedefing each signature shape rather than casting to raw untyped function pointers at every call.
Pointer sentinel checks from assembly may need integer casts such as uintptr_t to match behavior and avoid misleading C types.
Matching PRs can include header signature fixes when the new decompiled code reveals a more general parameter type than the old declaration.
naming_conventions: Stage animation functions use the module prefix grAnime_ followed by the original address, e.g. grAnime_801C6C0C.
Unidentified local/static functions remain fn_801Cxxxx when no semantic name is established, as with fn_801C6EE4 and fn_801C6F2C.
Unidentified globals use module-address names, e.g. grAnime_804D6958 and grAnime_804D695C.
Temporary or unresolved structs are named conservatively, e.g. Foo and struct _unk_struct_pobj, rather than being given speculative domain names.
Callback typedefs in the new code are generic and signature-oriented: Callback1, Callback2, Callback3, Callback4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around grAnime_801C67A8 call context to influence inlining for matching.
Kept #if 0 alternatives around recursive/helper-call forms in grAnime_801C706C, grAnime_801C70E0, and grAnime_801C7228, suggesting helper abstraction did not always produce matching code.

Used explicit temporary variables such as phi_r31, phi_r30, phi_r23, phi_r24, temp_r20, and temp_r4 in the harder 90%-range function grAnime_801C7228, preserving decompiler-like control flow where needed.
Used a linked-list pointer-to-pointer rewrite in grAnime_801C69FC to move the first FObj with obj_type 0xC to the front of an AObj fobj list.
Used enum-derived bit masks CALL_ON_DOBJ, CALL_ON_MOBJ, CALL_ON_POBJ, and CALL_ON_TOBJ, but still retained raw constants like 0x20, 0x200, and 0x400 where matching or unknown semantics required it.
Introduced exact callback argument type casts and dereferences in grAnime_801C6F50 to match multiple switch cases for float and int parameter payloads.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type Foo in granime.c.
Find later PRs that replace struct _unk_struct_pobj or pobj->u.unk with a semantic PObj shape-animation structure.
Search for later improvements to grAnime_801C6A54, grAnime_801C6C0C, and grAnime_801C7228, since the PR body reports these as partial matches rather than 100%.
Search whether raw flags 0x20, 0x200, 0x400, 0x1000, and 0x4020 were later given named constants.
Check whether grAnime_804D6958 and grAnime_804D695C receive semantic names in a subsequent cleanup.
